sources.title: Blackstone Inc. Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Core annual filing: business overview, segment reporting (Real Estate, PE, Credit & Insurance, Hedge Fund Solutions), MD&A, risk factors. Total AUM ~$1.1T at year-end 2024.

sources.title: Blackstone Q4 & Full Year 2024 Earnings Press Release and Supplement
sources.relevance_notes: One of the best quarters in history per CEO Schwarzman. Key drivers: inflows, investment activity, and realizations all reached highest levels in 2.5 years. Detailed segment financial data.

sources.title: Blackstone Q4 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
sources.relevance_notes: Management Q&A on strategic priorities, fundraising pipeline, deployment opportunities, perpetual capital vehicles growth, and competitive positioning.

sources.title: Blackstone Q2 2025 Earnings Press Release and Supplement
sources.relevance_notes: Mid-year 2025 strategic update with segment-level financials, fundraising progress, and deployment activity.

sources.title: Blackstone Investor Presentation (2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Strategic overview presentation covering platform diversification, perpetual capital growth, credit & insurance expansion, and data center/AI infrastructure thesis.

sources.title: Goldman Sachs U.S. Financial Services Conference -- Blackstone Presentation
sources.relevance_notes: Senior management presentation at major industry conference. Strategic commentary on alternatives growth, private wealth channel, and competitive moats.

sources.title: KKR & Co. Inc. Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Core annual filing: Asset Management, Insurance (Global Atlantic), Strategic Holdings segments. AUM at $638B. FRE, Total Operating Earnings, Adjusted Net Income all grew 35%+ YoY.

sources.title: KKR 2024 Investor Day Presentation
sources.relevance_notes: Comprehensive strategic presentation. Path to $1T+ AUM in 5 years. Strategic Holdings operating earnings projected $300M+ by 2026, $1B+ by 2030. Capital markets business reached $1B revenue milestone. Core PE strategy details.

sources.title: KKR Q4 & Full Year 2024 Earnings Results and Overview Presentation
sources.relevance_notes: Record year: raised $100B+ capital, record investment activity, $1B capital markets revenue for first time. Segment-level financial detail.

sources.title: KKR Q4 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
sources.relevance_notes: Management commentary on Global Atlantic integration, insurance strategy, private wealth channel expansion, and capital markets growth trajectory.

sources.title: KKR Investor Presentation (May 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Periodic investor update with strategic positioning, AUM breakdown, fundraising pipeline, and Strategic Holdings growth metrics.

sources.title: KKR Strategic Holdings -- Ownership Increase Announcement ($1.1B)
sources.relevance_notes: Increased ownership in USI Insurance Services, 1-800 Contacts, Heartland Dental. Signals strategic commitment to Core PE / permanent capital model.

sources.title: Apollo Global Management Inc. Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Core annual filing: Asset Management + Retirement Services (Athene) segments. Comprehensive business overview, strategy articulation, risk factors.

sources.title: Apollo 2024 Investor Day Presentation
sources.relevance_notes: Comprehensive strategic vision. Target to double AUM to $1.5T in 5 years. Athene retirement services growth strategy. Origination platform at $300B+ record. Credit-first model differentiation.

sources.title: Apollo Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Press Release
sources.relevance_notes: FY2025: record origination >$300B, inflows >$225B, record FRE and SRE. Adjusted net income $5.2B (+14% YoY), $8.38/share.

sources.title: Apollo Q1 2025 Earnings Press Release
sources.relevance_notes: Q1 2025: record FRE driven by strong management fee growth. Quarterly data on origination volumes and deployment.

sources.title: Apollo Q2 2025 Earnings Press Release
sources.relevance_notes: Record quarterly FRE of $627M. Combined FRE and SRE totaled $1.4B. Continued strong management fee growth.

sources.title: Disruption Banking Analysis: Apollo's $5.9B Earnings in 2025
sources.relevance_notes: Independent analysis of Apollo's FY2025 earnings. Third-party perspective on strategic positioning and credit-first model.

sources.title: Ares Management Corporation Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Core annual filing: Credit, PE, Real Assets, Secondaries, Other segments. Record $93B raised in 2024, AUM $484B at year-end.

sources.title: Ares Management Investor Day Presentation (May 2024)
sources.relevance_notes: AUM target >$750B by 2028 (75%+ increase from $419B at end-2023). FRE CAGR 16-20% to $2.65B midpoint. Realized income CAGR 20-25% to $3.5B. Dividend CAGR 20%.

sources.title: Ares Management 2024 Annual Letter (CEO Letter to Shareholders)
sources.relevance_notes: Strategic narrative from CEO Michael Arougheti. Record fundraising, AUM milestones, credit platform dominance, and growth strategy articulation.

sources.title: Ares Management Q4 & FY2024 Earnings Press Release
sources.relevance_notes: FY2024 financial results: record fundraising ($93B), AUM $484B, segment-level detail on Credit, PE, Real Assets, Secondaries.

sources.title: Ares Investor Presentation (Q3 2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Periodic investor update: AUM breakdown by strategy, fundraising pipeline, credit market positioning, and competitive differentiation.

sources.title: Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Core annual filing post-corporate restructuring (BN transferred 73% interest to BAM in Feb 2025). Total fundraising >$135B in 2024. Fee-Bearing Capital, DE, and FRE metrics.

sources.title: Brookfield Asset Management Record 2025 Results Press Release
sources.relevance_notes: Record 2025 results: Revenue $4.8B, Fee-Bearing Capital $603B, DE $2.7B. 15% dividend increase. Fundraised $100B+ in 2025 with 50 funds in market for 2026.

sources.title: Brookfield Asset Management Investor Presentation (May 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Strategic overview: platform diversification across RE, Infrastructure, PE, Credit, Insurance Solutions. Fee-bearing capital growth trajectory.

sources.title: Brookfield Asset Management -- Goldman Sachs U.S. Financial Services Conference
sources.relevance_notes: Strategic commentary: raised $120B in 2024, $100B in 2025, expects larger fundraising in 2026 with 50 funds in market. Angel Oak acquisition. Wealth channel expansion.

sources.title: Brookfield Asset Management -- Angel Oak Acquisition Announcement
sources.relevance_notes: Acquisition expanding credit capabilities. Signals strategic push into securitized credit and mortgage markets.

sources.title: Blue Owl Capital Inc. Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Core annual filing: Credit, GP Strategic Capital, Real Estate platforms. AUM >$250B at year-end 2024. ~91% of management fees from Permanent Capital vehicles.

sources.title: Blue Owl Capital 2025 Investor Day Presentation
sources.relevance_notes: Five-year strategic outlook. Presentations by senior management on growth strategy, permanent capital model differentiation, product innovation, and M&A integration.

sources.title: Blue Owl Capital Q4 2024 Earnings Results
sources.relevance_notes: Record fundraising and deployment in 2024. Q4 financial results including management fees, FRE, and DE by segment.

sources.title: Blue Owl Capital Q3 2025 Investor Presentation
sources.relevance_notes: Updated AUM, fundraising, and deployment metrics. Platform growth and diversification progress vs. investor day targets.

sources.title: Blue Owl Capital -- Strategic M&A Announcements (2024-2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Multiple strategic acquisitions to expand platform. Product innovation through M&A a key differentiator cited by Co-CEOs Ostrover and Lipschultz.

sources.title: TPG Inc. Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Core annual filing: Capital, Growth, Impact (Rise/Rise Climate), Real Estate, Market Solutions segments. AUM grew 173% from $90B (2020) to $245.9B (2024).

sources.title: TPG Q4 & Full Year 2024 Earnings Results
sources.relevance_notes: FY2024 results with earnings release, financial supplement, and transcript. AUM $245.9B. Segment-level financial data.

sources.title: TPG Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings Results
sources.relevance_notes: FY2025 results: FRE surged 25% to $953M, FRE margin expanded to 52%. Rise Climate Fund progress. Full-year segment performance.

sources.title: TPG Rise Climate Fund II Fundraising Updates
sources.relevance_notes: Rise Climate Fund II: $6.2B committed, $5.8B closed (vs. $10B target/cap). Techem acquisition (EUR 6.7B) as anchor deal. Climate/impact investing differentiation.

sources.title: TPG Investor Presentations and Financial Supplements (2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Quarterly investor presentations with AUM by segment, fundraising progress, deployment activity, and margin trajectory.

sources.title: The Carlyle Group Inc. Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Core annual filing: Global PE, Global Credit, Global Investment Solutions segments. Record FRE >$1.1B (+30% YoY), FRE margin 46% (+900bps). Total inflows $41B.

sources.title: Carlyle 2024 Annual Report
sources.relevance_notes: CEO Harvey Schwartz narrative: platform strength, disciplined execution, 2-year inflows >$100B. Strategic positioning for growth under new leadership.

sources.title: Carlyle Q4 & Full Year 2024 Earnings Press Release
sources.relevance_notes: FY2024: met/exceeded all financial targets. Record FRE >$1.1B. Direct lending volume doubled YoY (>$300B). CEO Schwartz, CFO Redett commentary.

sources.title: Carlyle 2028 Financial Targets Presentation
sources.relevance_notes: Medium-term targets: $200B+ capital raise, $1.9B FRE, $6/share DE by 2028. Strategic growth path under CEO Schwartz.

sources.title: Bloomberg: Carlyle Targets $200B in New Inflows as Schwartz Sets Growth Path
sources.relevance_notes: Independent reporting on Carlyle's medium-term targets. Third-party perspective on CEO Schwartz's strategic turnaround narrative.

sources.title: EQT AB Annual and Sustainability Report 2024
sources.relevance_notes: Comprehensive annual report covering 5 years as listed company. Expanded from 6 to 18 investment strategies since 2019 IPO. EBITDA EUR 1,359M (58% margin). Fee-related EBITDA EUR 1,108M (53% margin).

sources.title: EQT AB Capital Markets Day 2024 Presentation
sources.relevance_notes: Strategic CMD in Stockholm. Revenue grown 4x since listing. EBITDA margin expanded from 46% to 58%. Market cap up ~470%. Medium- to long-term financial targets. Thematic investment strategy evolution.

sources.title: EQT AB Year-End Report 2025
sources.relevance_notes: FY2025: EPS EUR 1.123. Gross inflows EUR 26B (>2x the EUR 11B in 2024). Record investment activity. Closed largest PE fund globally.

sources.title: EQT AB Year-End Report 2024
sources.relevance_notes: FY2024: returned to record levels of investments, increased exit activity. Baseline year for YoY comparisons.

sources.title: EQT AB Capital Markets Event in New York
sources.relevance_notes: U.S.-focused investor event. Strategic positioning for North American LPs. Growth plans and U.S. market opportunity.

sources.title: Partners Group Annual Report 2024
sources.relevance_notes: Comprehensive annual report. AUM USD 152B at end-2024. Direct investments: $3.7B PE, $1.4B RE, $3.6B Infrastructure, $4.2B Credit. Swiss-listed (SIX: PGHN).

sources.title: Partners Group Investor Relations Presentation (May 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Mid-year investor presentation with strategic overview, AUM breakdown, direct investment approach, and evergreen/bespoke solutions growth.

sources.title: Partners Group H1 2025 Business Update & AUM Announcement
sources.relevance_notes: H1 2025 AUM update. Client demand and fundraising pipeline. Management fee and performance fee guidance.

sources.title: Partners Group FY2025 Annual Results -- AUM Reaches USD 185B
sources.relevance_notes: FY2025: AUM USD 185B (up from $152B). Commitments USD 26.2B (above guidance). Bespoke solutions 72% of fundraising. Record mandate solutions and evergreen years. 2026 guidance: $26-32B new demand.

sources.title: Partners Group Interim Report 2025
sources.relevance_notes: H1 2025 detailed financials. Management fees growth, performance fee outlook, and strategic partnership announcements (Deutsche Bank, PGIM, Generali, Lincoln Financial, BBVA, Erste, Perpetual).

sources.title: StepStone Group Inc. Form 10-K Annual Report (FY ended March 31, 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Core annual filing. Total capital responsibility ~$709B including $189B AUM. Advisory/solutions-focused platform. Fiscal year ends March 31.

sources.title: StepStone Group Q4 & Full Fiscal Year 2025 Earnings Results
sources.relevance_notes: FY2025 (ended March 2025): $189B AUM, ~$709B total capital. Regular + supplemental dividends declared. Segment-level financial data.

sources.title: StepStone Group Quarterly Earnings Presentations (FY2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Quarterly presentations covering AUM growth, advisory/discretionary split, fundraising for underlying GP clients, and private wealth distribution.

sources.title: StepStone Group Form 10-Q (Q2 FY2026, ended September 30, 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Most recent quarterly filing with updated AUM, revenue, and operating metrics for the period.

sources.title: Hamilton Lane Inc. Form 10-K Annual Report (FY ended March 31, 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Core annual filing. AUM $138B (+11% YoY). Fee-earning AUM $72B (+10%). Management/advisory fees $513.9M (+14%). GAAP EPS $5.41. Fiscal year ends March 31.

sources.title: Hamilton Lane Q4 & Full Fiscal Year 2025 Earnings Results
sources.relevance_notes: FY2025 results: total AUM $138B, management fees +14%, GAAP net income $217.4M. Presentation materials and webcast.

sources.title: Hamilton Lane Shareholder Day 2024
sources.relevance_notes: Annual shareholder day with strategic overview. Presentation materials posted. Webcast replay available. Private markets platform strategy and growth trajectory.

sources.title: Hamilton Lane 2025 Market Overview: Private Markets Reach an Inflection Point
sources.relevance_notes: Annual market overview leveraging 58,000+ fund database across 57 vintage years. Industry outlook plus Hamilton Lane's positioning. Evergreen fund growth thesis.

sources.title: Hamilton Lane 2025 Mid-Year Market Update
sources.relevance_notes: Mid-year outlook on private markets. Infrastructure and credit opportunity assessment. Evergreen fund growth projections. AUM/supervision: $956B total ($135B discretionary, $821B non-discretionary).

sources.title: Brookfield Corporation Form 40-F Annual Report (FY2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Parent company of BAM. Annual filing with SEC and Canadian regulators. Distributable earnings $4.9B ($3.07/share, +15%). Asset management, wealth solutions, operating businesses segments.

sources.title: Brookfield Corporation Record 2024 Results Press Release
sources.relevance_notes: Record results: DE before realizations $1.5B/quarter, $4.9B/year (+15%/share YoY). Asset management inflows >$135B. Wealth solutions established as top-tier U.S. annuity writer.

sources.title: Brookfield Corporation 2024 Annual Report (CEO Letter)
sources.relevance_notes: CEO Bruce Flatt's letter to shareholders. Strategic narrative covering asset management, insurance/wealth solutions, operating businesses. Long-term value creation philosophy.

sources.title: Brookfield Corporation Q3 2025 Earnings Press Release
sources.relevance_notes: Q3 2025 results: strong continued performance. Transaction activity expected to accelerate. Cash flow and intrinsic value growth trajectory.

sources.title: Brookfield Corporation -- Corporate Restructuring Completion (BAM Transfer)
sources.relevance_notes: BN transferred ~73% interest in Asset Management Company to BAM. BAM market cap now reflects 100% of asset management value. Key structural change for peer comparison.

sources.title: ICG Annual Report and Accounts FY2025 (Year Ended March 31, 2025)
sources.relevance_notes: Full annual report. AUM $112B, fee-earning AUM $75B (+8% YoY, 14% 5-year CAGR). $24B raised. Management fees GBP 604M (+19%). Dry powder $32B. LSE: ICP.

sources.title: ICG Final Results for FY Ended March 31, 2025
sources.relevance_notes: FY2025 results announcement: world's largest GP-led secondaries fund (Strategic Equity V), Europe's largest direct lending fundraise (SDP V), largest vintage-to-vintage upsize (Europe Mid-Market II, 3x).

sources.title: ICG Annual Report and Accounts FY2024 (Year Ended March 31, 2024)
sources.relevance_notes: Prior year annual report for trend comparison. Strategic positioning and growth trajectory baseline.

sources.title: ICG Interim Results (H1 FY2025, Six Months Ended September 30, 2024)
sources.relevance_notes: H1 FY2025 interim results: PBT GBP 182.8M. APM basis PBT GBP 198.4M. Mid-year fundraising and deployment update.

sources.title: ICG Shareholder Events and Capital Markets Day Recordings
sources.relevance_notes: Recordings of live events, presentations, and seminars. Capital markets day materials with strategic outlook.

sources.title: AssetMark Financial Holdings Form 10-K Annual Report (FY2023)
sources.relevance_notes: Last publicly available annual report before going private. Wealth management technology platform serving >9,200 financial advisors, >261,000 investor households. Platform assets ~$119B.

sources.title: AssetMark Completes Acquisition by GTCR (Going Private)
sources.relevance_notes: GTCR acquired 100% of AMK at $35.25/share (~$2.7B equity value). Stock no longer publicly traded. New chairman Lou Maiuri appointed. NOTE: Public source availability now very limited.

sources.title: AssetMark Q2 2024 Earnings Results (Last Public Quarterly Report)
sources.relevance_notes: Last public quarterly report: platform assets $119.4B. Net income $32.3M ($0.43/share). Adj. net income $49.8M ($0.66/share). Revenue $198.5M. Baseline before going private.

industry_wide_sources.title: Bain & Company: Global Private Equity Report 2025
industry_wide_sources.relevance_notes: Annual PE industry benchmark. Alternatives fundraising ~$1.3T. Buyout fundraising -16%. Individual investors = 50% of global capital but only 16% of alternatives AUM. Sovereign wealth and individual investors to drive ~60% of AUM growth over next decade.

industry_wide_sources.title: Bain & Company: Global Private Equity Report 2026
industry_wide_sources.relevance_notes: Most recent edition. PE deal value +14% to $2T in 2024 (3rd highest on record). Large buyouts >$500M surged 37%. Median PE purchase multiple rose to 11.8x. 16,000+ companies held >4 years (52% of buyout inventory, highest ever). Exit value rebounded 41% to $1.3T.

industry_wide_sources.title: McKinsey Global Private Markets Report 2025: Braced for Shifting Weather
industry_wide_sources.relevance_notes: Comprehensive private markets analysis. Closed-end PE fundraising -17% globally. Infrastructure highlighted as top allocation increase (46% of LPs). First time since 2015 that distributions exceeded contributions. North American fundraising +8%.

industry_wide_sources.title: Preqin Global Reports 2025: Alternatives in 2025
industry_wide_sources.relevance_notes: Covers six alternative asset classes. Global alternatives AUM forecast to reach $32T by 2030. Optimistic sentiment on dealmaking recovery. LP allocation trends across PE, credit, infrastructure, RE, hedge funds, natural resources.

industry_wide_sources.title: CalPERS Private Equity Annual Program Review (FY2024-25)
industry_wide_sources.relevance_notes: World's largest public pension PE program review. PE allocation increased from 13% to 17%. Co-investment scaling. Large/Mega buyout commitment share fell from 73% to 43%. Manager selection and GP evaluation context.

industry_wide_sources.title: CalSTRS Private Equity Semi-Annual Executive Summary (September 2025)
industry_wide_sources.relevance_notes: CalSTRS PE NAV $56.2B (16.1% of total fund). PE portfolio outperformed custom index across all periods. Co-investments now >24% of portfolio. Manager evaluation and GP relationship insights.

industry_wide_sources.title: CPP Investments Fiscal Year 2025 Annual Report
industry_wide_sources.relevance_notes: CPPIB net assets C$714.4B. 9.3% annual return. PE portfolio $156B. Plans ~C$20B annual PE deployment. Committed EUR 500M to EQT Infrastructure Fund VI, additional to Blackstone RE Partners Europe VII. Manager relationship and allocation insights.

coverage_assessment.gaps_identified: 1. AssetMark (AMK) went private via GTCR acquisition in September 2024; only pre-acquisition public filings available (FY2023 10-K, Q2 2024 earnings). No post-acquisition public disclosures. Source coverage is minimal but reflects true data availability. 2. EQT AB (EQT) is Swedish-listed on Nasdaq Stockholm, not a U.S. SEC filer; filings are via Swedish regulatory framework (not 10-K/10-Q). Annual report and year-end reports serve equivalent purpose. 3. ICG (ICP) is UK-listed on LSE with March 31 fiscal year-end. Filings are IFRS-based annual reports, not SEC 10-K. 4. Partners Group (PGHN) is Swiss-listed on SIX. Reports under Swiss GAAP/IFRS, not SEC-filed. 5. Sell-side equity research reports for all firms are paywalled; coverage relies on public earnings, investor day materials, and press releases. 6. Non-obvious sources (pension fund board docs) provide strong independent validation for large-cap GPs (BX, KKR, APO, ARES, CG, TPG, EQT, BAM) but do not typically discuss smaller managers (STEP, HLN, OWL, ICP, PGHN, AMK). 7. Job posting data and patent/trademark filings were not systematically cataloged in this stage; could be added in VD-A2 data collection.
